ksa_text: Skill in conducting AI conformity assessments under the EU AI Act, including risk classification determination, technical documentation evaluation, and compliance assessment against applicable high-risk AI system requirements.

ddat.top_element_label: Capability building for digital, data and technology

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

onet.top_element_label: Evaluating Information to Determine Compliance with Standards

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
Capability building for digital, data and technology

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Included: skill maps directly to AI Incident Response Manager scope — RSK-specific signals detected ("risk classif", "compliance assess", "conformity.{0,10}assess"). STRM composite is strong (0.6000); note that framework scores routinely underweight risk management and governance vocabulary, so text-based signal carries primary weight here. Seniority is appropriate: the KSA describes practice-level execution, not enterprise strategy.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: External check: no matches in RSK job postings or cert curricula (CRISC, FRM, NIST AI RMF, CISM). Would a working AI Incident Response Manager self-report this as required?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Challenge answered: the competency is implicit in AI Incident Response Manager work (domain affinity 0.65, 4 RSK signal(s)). Framework vocabulary matching systematically underrepresents risk management and governance KSAs.
